data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN (2015, updated 2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; State Forest Portal

Q2.v: Forest Department, Government of Chhattisgarh; implementation through District Forest Officers
Q2.src: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6

Q3.v: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN (establishing notification and 2024 update); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 (Demand for Grants No. 6, Forest Department); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25
Q3.src: programs.md — BUDGET, SURVEY, OUTCOME, ANNOUNCEMENT, CAG, PORTAL entries

Q4.r: The scheme explicitly and exclusively targets private landholdings. Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN specifies 'Target: 3.5 lakh trees/year on private land.' Eligibility requires min 0.5 ha (fruit) / 2 ha (timber) private landholding. Economic Survey 2024-25 confirms 'tree plantations on private land.' CAG 2024-25 confirms 42,000 registered farmers with private holdings. All plantation activity, incentive payments, and sapling distribution flow to and remain on private property. This is definitively Type C — private land. AUTOMATIC TEST 1 FAIL triggered.
Q4.src: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q5.v: The primary resource is private agricultural/forestry land held by 42,000 registered farmers across Chhattisgarh state. Approximate coverage: 22,500 hectares (calculated at 1,867 trees/hectare, cumulative since 2015). Minimum holding eligibility: 0.5 hectare (fruit tree plantation) or 2 hectare (timber plantation). Species planted: 185 approved varieties (fruit, timber, fodder). No fixed geographic boundary — scheme is state-wide, operating across districts. Individual parcels are private property with no commons demarcation.
Q5.src: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q6.r: No commons resource exists to demarcate. The scheme operates on private land parcels. Individual farmer parcels are registered in the scheme portal (42,000 enrolled), but this constitutes private land records, not a public commons boundary document. No GP resolution, watershed boundary map, survey number for a commons resource, or equivalent public commons demarcation document exists or is applicable.
Q6.src: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN; State Forest Portal

Q7.r: Farmers hold statutory freehold property rights to their private land under applicable state revenue law and registration records. Score = 2 (statutory/legally enforceable right). NOTE: This is moot — T1.1 = C (private land) triggers automatic T1 FAIL regardless of T1.2 score. The T1.2 pass/fail assessment is inapplicable to private land under the SOC framework, which requires community access to a commons. Recorded for completeness.
Q7.src: State revenue law (private land title); Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN (eligibility for landholders)

Q8.r: No active diversion order, mining lease, SEZ notification, compulsory acquisition, or equivalent override process affecting the scheme's operational land exists. The land is private property; no evidence of active legal encumbrance found in available records. CAG 2024-25 confirms continued scheme operation without disruption. However, this pass is moot given T1.1 = C automatic fail.
Q8.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN

Q9.breakdown: Tree sapling provision: ₹45 cr; Plantation incentive (₹1,000/tree × 3.5 lakh trees): ₹35 cr; Monitoring: ₹15 cr
Q9.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department)

Q10.r: All scheme expenditure flows to private land and private actors. (1) Sapling provision (₹45 cr): saplings are sourced from certified nurseries and distributed to private farmers for planting on private land — outputs flow exclusively to private assets. (2) Plantation incentive (₹35 cr): direct cash transfer to individual private landholders at ₹1,000/tree. (3) Monitoring (₹15 cr): administrative overhead for tracking survival on private land — no commons benefit created. No expenditure component creates or maintains a commons resource accessible to the broader community. Per the framework, expenditure whose outputs flow exclusively to private actors is excluded from the numerator even if physically located near public land.
Q10.src: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 (Demand for Grants No. 6); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q11.r: Total scheme expenditure of ₹95 cr (FY 2025-26) constitutes private-benefit spend: ₹45 cr saplings distributed to private farmers for private land planting; ₹35 cr cash incentive to private landholders; ₹15 cr monitoring of private plantations. Q10 = 0, Q11 = 95, sum = 95 = Q9. Rationale for Q11 = 100% of Q9: scheme design is explicitly a private land greening incentive with no commons component.
Q11.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 (Demand for Grants No. 6); Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN

Q12.interp: FAIL — θ₁ = 0.00, well below the 0.50 threshold. Entire scheme expenditure benefits private land assets.
Q12.src: Derived from Q9 and Q10 above

docs: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN (July 1 2015, updated 2024) — scheme guidelines, eligibility, incentive structure
Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department) — ₹95 cr budget breakdown
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — ₹420 cr cumulative disbursed, 42,000 farmers, expenditure verification
Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — program performance data
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — verified targets and achievements

Q14.r: Benefits are fully excludable and accrue only to the individual private landholder. The tree (physical asset), the cash incentive (₹1,000/tree), and the maintenance allowance (₹500/tree/year) all accrue exclusively to the registered farmer on whose private land the plantation occurs. No other community member can access these benefits. While CO2 sequestration is a diffuse environmental benefit, it is not the scheme's primary resource benefit and is not operationalised as a community livelihood or access right. Score = 0 (fully excludable). Automatic T1 FAIL additionally triggered by this sub-parameter.
Q14.src: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN (eligibility: individual landholders); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 (individual farmer incentive payments)

pass_verification.C1_T1.1: FAIL — T1.1 = C (private land/property). Automatic Test 1 FAIL; no community access or commons governance exists.
pass_verification.C2_T1.2: PASS — T1.2 = 2 (statutory property rights of private landowners, score ≥ 1). NOTE: moot given T1.1 = C automatic fail; T1.2 does not rescue a C classification.
pass_verification.C3_theta1: FAIL — θ₁ = 0.00, below 0.50 threshold. No expenditure on non-private commons resources.
pass_verification.C4_T1.4: FAIL — T1.4 = 0 (fully excludable, benefits to individual private landholder only).
pass_verification.C5_T1.6: PASS — No active legal encumbrance on private land found.

sources_verified: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6
